Description: Write unit tests in a #[cfg(test)] module alongside the code being tested
Setup: Add #[cfg(test)] mod tests { use super::*; } at the bottom of each source file
Common Mistake: Forgetting #[cfg(test)] annotation which includes test code in production binary; putting tests in a separate file for private functions

Description: Use the correct assert macro for clear failure messages
Common Mistake: Using assert!(a == b) instead of assert_eq!(a, b) which shows both values on failure; not providing custom messages for unclear assertions

Description: Organize unit tests in inline modules and integration tests in tests/ directory
Setup: Unit tests: #[cfg(test)] mod in source. Integration tests: tests/ directory at crate root. Examples: examples/ directory
Common Mistake: Putting unit tests in tests/ directory where they cannot access private functions; putting integration tests inside src/ where they can access internals

Description: Create shared test setup using helper functions and Drop for cleanup
Setup: Define struct with test state; implement setup function; use Drop for cleanup
Common Mistake: Not cleaning up test resources; using a shared database between tests causing flaky behavior; hardcoding test data that conflicts between parallel tests

Description: Test async code with a test-specific Tokio runtime
Setup: Add tokio as a dev-dependency with macros and rt features
Common Mistake: Using #[test] with block_on manually; spawning a new runtime inside each test; not using multi_thread flavor when testing concurrent code

Description: Generate random test inputs to find edge cases that manual tests miss
Common Mistake: Only testing happy-path examples; not using property testing for parsers and serializers where roundtrip properties should hold

Description: Capture complex output as snapshot files and detect regressions by comparing against saved snapshots
Setup: Add insta as a dev-dependency; run cargo insta review to approve changes
Common Mistake: Manually comparing large JSON/string outputs with assert_eq; not using cargo insta review to manage snapshot updates

Description: Generate mock implementations of traits for isolated unit testing
Setup: Define traits for dependencies; add mockall as dev-dependency; use #[automock] or mock! macro
Common Mistake: Mocking concrete types instead of traits; not verifying expected call counts; using mocks when a simple test stub would be clearer

Description: Spin up real database and service containers for integration tests using testcontainers
Setup: Add testcontainers as a dev-dependency; ensure Docker is available in the test environment
Common Mistake: Using a shared test database instead of isolated containers; not cleaning up containers; hardcoding ports that may conflict

Description: Test that functions return expected errors or panic as documented
Setup: Use #[should_panic] for panic tests; return Result from tests for error assertions
Common Mistake: Not testing error paths; using #[should_panic] without the expected message; testing only the happy path

Description: Use criterion for statistical benchmarks with confidence intervals and regression detection
Setup: Add criterion as a dev-dependency; create benches/ directory; hook up bench harness in Cargo.toml
Common Mistake: Using #[bench] (nightly only) for benchmarks; using Instant::now() for microbenchmarks (noisy, no statistics); not using black_box to prevent optimization

Description: Use rstest for parameterized tests and reusable fixtures
Common Mistake: Writing separate test functions for each input case; duplicating test setup across many tests

Description: Capture and assert on tracing events in tests
Setup: Use tracing-test crate for capturing spans and events
Common Mistake: Not testing log output; using println! instead of tracing in application code

Description: Verify that incorrect usage of APIs and macros produces expected compiler errors
Setup: Add trybuild as a dev-dependency; create test cases in tests/ui/ directory
Common Mistake: Not testing compile-time errors for proc macros; relying solely on documentation to communicate API constraints

Description: Measure test coverage to identify untested code paths
Code Example:
# Using cargo-llvm-cov (recommended):
# cargo install cargo-llvm-cov
# cargo llvm-cov --html --open

# Using tarpaulin:
# cargo install cargo-tarpaulin
# cargo tarpaulin --out Html

# In CI (GitHub Actions):
# - uses: taiki-e/install-action@cargo-llvm-cov
# - run: cargo llvm-cov --lcov --output-path lcov.info
Common Mistake: Targeting 100% coverage which leads to testing implementation details; not measuring coverage at all; only testing happy paths

Description: Write doc tests that serve as both documentation examples and tests
Setup: Add code examples in /// doc comments; use # to hide boilerplate lines
Common Mistake: Not writing doc tests for public APIs; writing doc tests that do not compile; using ```ignore instead of fixing the example

Description: Fuzz functions that parse untrusted input to find crashes and security bugs
Setup: Install cargo-fuzz; create fuzz targets in fuzz/ directory
Common Mistake: Not fuzzing parsers and deserializers; only testing with known good inputs; ignoring cargo-fuzz findings

Keywords: test helper, assert, custom macro, pretty
Description: Create reusable test assertion helpers and custom macros
Setup: Define helpers in a test utilities module or use similar-asserts crate
Common Mistake: Using assert_eq! with large structs that produce unreadable diff output; duplicating assertion logic across tests
